title: 5.	Do any national policies, strategies or action plans o...

messages.content: 5.	Do any national policies, strategies or action plans other than the National Wetland Policy recognize the value of wetlands?

.

Final Answer:

Beyond the **National Wetland Policy**, several other national policies, strategies, and action plans in Sri Lanka acknowledge and emphasize the value of wetlands. These documents demonstrate a broader recognition of wetlands' importance for environmental sustainability and national development.

The **National Environmental Policy** (p. 5) explicitly recognizes the ecological functions of wetlands, including flood control, water purification, and biodiversity conservation as vital for sustainable development. Furthermore, it prioritizes the conservation and sustainable management of critical ecosystems, including wetlands, mangroves, and coral reefs (p. 8) [National Environmental Policy, p. 5-8].

The **Sri Lanka Biodiversity Strategy Action Plan** identifies wetlands as a priority ecosystem for conservation due to their high biodiversity value and critical role in providing ecosystem services such as water regulation and carbon sequestration (p. 7) [Sri Lanka Biodiversity Strategy Action Plan, p. 7].  Action 4.3 specifically calls for the development and implementation of wetland conservation and restoration programs (p. 12) [Sri Lanka Biodiversity Strategy Action Plan, p. 12].

The **Coastal Zone Management Plan** recognizes coastal wetlands, including lagoons, mangroves, and salt marshes, as vital components of the coastal ecosystem, providing habitat and coastal protection (p. 3) [Coastal Zone Management Plan, p. 3]. The plan’s strategies prioritize the conservation and sustainable use of these coastal wetlands (p. 6) [Coastal Zone Management Plan, p. 6].

Finally, the **National Policy on Climate Change** highlights the crucial role of wetlands in climate change mitigation through carbon sequestration and adaptation by providing natural flood protection (p. 9) [National Policy on Climate Change, p. 9]. It promotes the restoration and sustainable management of wetlands to enhance their resilience to climate change impacts (p. 11) [National Policy on Climate Change, p. 11].

In conclusion, these diverse national frameworks collectively underscore the significant ecological, economic, and social value of wetlands in Sri Lanka, demonstrating a commitment to their conservation and sustainable management across various sectors.

**Sources Used:**
- National Environmental Policy, p. 5-8
- Sri Lanka Biodiversity Strategy Action Plan, p. 7, 12
- Coastal Zone Management Plan, p. 3, 6
- National Policy on Climate Change, p. 9, 11
